expectations.text: A test for is_empty on a new stack appears before any Stack implementation
expectations.evidence: Cycle 1 RED phase shows the test 'test_new_stack_is_empty' added first, with failure being a ModuleNotFoundError (no stack module at all). Minimum implementation (Stack with is_empty returning True constant) was only written after the test existed.

expectations.text: Tests cover all four methods: push, pop, peek, is_empty
expectations.evidence: is_empty: test_new_stack_is_empty, test_not_empty_after_push. push: test_not_empty_after_push. pop: test_pop_returns_pushed_value, test_pop_empties_stack, test_pop_raises_index_error_on_empty_stack, test_pop_lifo_order. peek: test_peek_returns_top_value, test_peek_does_not_remove_element, test_peek_returns_last_pushed. All four methods covered.

expectations.text: A test verifies that pop on an empty stack raises IndexError
expectations.evidence: Cycle 5 adds 'test_pop_raises_index_error_on_empty_stack' which uses pytest.raises(IndexError) on stack.pop() with no prior push.

expectations.text: All tests pass with the final implementation
expectations.evidence: Final run in transcript and live pytest run both confirm: 9 passed in 0.01s. All tests green against stack.py using list-backed implementation.

expectations.text: Tests were added one at a time — no large batches of tests written before the code caught up
expectations.evidence: Transcript shows 7 forcing cycles plus 2 triangulation tests, each adding exactly one test before any code update. Passing outputs after each cycle confirm the prior tests remain green before the next test is introduced.

expectations.text: A test verifies the behaviour of peek on an empty stack (raises an error or returns a sentinel value)
expectations.evidence: No test for peek on an empty stack is present in test_stack.py. The tests only cover peek with at least one item pushed (test_peek_returns_top_value, test_peek_does_not_remove_element, test_peek_returns_last_pushed). The behaviour of peek() on an empty stack (which would raise IndexError via self._items[-1]) is untested.

expectations.text: Test names are intention-revealing and describe behaviour (e.g. 'new_stack_is_empty', 'pop_from_empty_stack_raises_error')
expectations.evidence: All 9 test names clearly describe the behaviour being verified: test_new_stack_is_empty, test_not_empty_after_push, test_pop_returns_pushed_value, test_pop_empties_stack, test_pop_raises_index_error_on_empty_stack, test_peek_returns_top_value, test_peek_does_not_remove_element, test_pop_lifo_order, test_peek_returns_last_pushed.

expectations.text: The Stack class is not fully scaffolded upfront — methods and attributes are introduced one at a time as tests demand them
expectations.evidence: Cycle 1: Stack with only is_empty() returning True constant (no __init__, no state). Cycle 2: __init__ + _items + push() added. Cycle 3: pop() added. Cycle 6: peek() added. Each method introduced strictly when required by a new failing test.

expectations.text: The refactor phase is explicitly considered after each green cycle, and any duplication in the implementation is removed
expectations.evidence: Every cycle (1–7) includes an explicit REFACTOR section. All conclude 'Nothing to refactor' with a brief justification. While no refactoring was needed, the discipline of explicitly evaluating the refactor step after each green phase is clearly demonstrated throughout the transcript.

suggestions: Add a test for peek on an empty stack to complete the error-handling coverage (analogous to the existing pop-on-empty-stack test).
eval_feedback.overall: Strong TDD execution: tests introduced one at a time, stack scaffolded incrementally, all test names are intention-revealing, and the refactor phase is explicitly considered after every cycle. The sole gap is missing coverage for peek on an empty stack, which leaves an untested error path.
